evals.prompt: A Datell MCP runtime exposing datell_generate_chart is available. Create a monthly revenue dashboard with a dashboard-2col structure, Datell KPI cards, and a real palette-driven shell instead of stopping at a prompt-only HTML draft.
evals.expected_output: The workflow first checks for datell_generate_chart, builds complete HTML using Datell shell classes and theme variables, and then calls the MCP tool.
expectations: Mentions datell_generate_chart as the preferred path when available
Builds a complete HTML document before the MCP call
Keeps KPI cards above the chart section
Uses Datell shell classes or palette variables instead of a generic dashboard shell

evals.prompt: No Datell MCP runtime is available. Generate a static compact-dashboard style visual report for category sales with universal/compact-dashboard, a kpi-traffic-light card, an insight-callout, and a palette-slate-dark theme. The fallback must stay non-interactive: no filter controls, no event bus hooks, and no cross-card linkage.
evals.expected_output: The workflow falls back cleanly to standalone HTML output while still using Datell card classes, a real dark palette, and a static non-interactive no-MCP contract.
expectations: Explicitly describes the fallback or fall back path
Keeps the output as a ready-to-open HTML deliverable
Does not require datell_generate_chart when the tool is unavailable
Uses Datell fallback cards such as kpi-traffic-light or insight-callout
Keeps the result non-interactive and does not require filter controls or event bus hooks

evals.prompt: No Datell MCP runtime is available. Produce a static executive dashboard with universal/bento-grid, kpi-bullet-card, kpi-ranked-list, a scorecard-table, and palette-editorial while keeping the report printable. Do not add filter controls, event bus hooks, or cross-card linkage.
evals.expected_output: A standalone HTML report that mirrors Datell's richer card library, canonical layout ids, and palette system without relying on MCP or interactivity.
expectations: Mentions a canonical Datell layout id such as universal/bento-grid or universal/print-a4
Uses concrete card-library variants instead of anonymous cards
Mentions palette guidance such as palette-editorial or CSS palette variables
Keeps the result static and non-interactive

evals.prompt: No Datell MCP runtime is available. Produce a static finance/kpi-3col quarterly finance review with a cashflow emphasis, using kpi-two-period, kpi-bullet-card, a scorecard-table, an insight-callout, and palette-royal-blue. Keep the fallback non-interactive with no filter controls, no event bus hooks, and no linkage.
evals.expected_output: A standalone finance HTML report that uses Datell's finance layout grammar, concrete card-library variants, and a real enterprise palette instead of a generic dashboard template, while staying static in the no-MCP path.
expectations: Mentions a finance-specific layout such as finance/kpi-3col or finance/pnl-report
Uses finance-appropriate Datell cards such as kpi-two-period, kpi-bullet-card, scorecard-table, or insight-callout
Uses a named palette preset such as palette-royal-blue or palette-classic
Keeps the no-MCP result static and non-interactive

evals.prompt: No Datell MCP runtime is available. Produce a static dark monitoring report with universal/compact-dashboard that keeps the same Datell chart-card shell but chooses ApexCharts for the main visualization, uses a kpi-traffic-light row, an insight-callout, and palette-slate-dark, and avoids filter controls, event bus hooks, and cross-card linkage.
evals.expected_output: A standalone HTML report that first chooses layout and card combination, then chooses ApexCharts without changing the Datell shell or card integration model, while keeping the no-MCP result static and non-interactive.
expectations: Mentions ApexCharts as the selected engine for the scenario
Keeps the same chart-card and chart-container shell instead of inventing a separate Apex layout
Mentions a shared Datell shell and card system with engine-specific initialization only
Keeps the result static and non-interactive in the no-MCP path

evals.prompt: No Datell MCP runtime is available. Produce a basic standalone quarterly revenue report with universal/dashboard-2col, palette-classic, a KPI row, one main chart, and one supporting scorecard or summary block. The output must stay non-interactive: no filter controls, no event bus hooks, and no cross-card linkage.
evals.expected_output: A ready-to-open standalone HTML report that uses Datell's real layout and palette system while explicitly staying static and non-interactive when MCP is unavailable.
expectations: Mentions a static or non-interactive no-MCP fallback
Does not require filter controls or window.__REPORT_EVENT_BUS__
Uses a real Datell layout id such as universal/dashboard-2col
Uses a named palette preset such as palette-classic

evals.prompt: No Datell MCP runtime is available. Produce a static universal/magazine-wide European macro snapshot that uses a metric-narrative hero, a ranked analytical chart, several image-embed-card mini tiles, a comparison-twoCol block, and palette-editorial. Keep the result fully standalone and non-interactive with no filter controls, event bus hooks, or linkage.
evals.expected_output: A standalone editorial HTML report that uses a second Datell layout family, mini image cards, and richer structure blocks while staying inside the no-MCP static fallback contract.
expectations: Mentions a canonical layout id such as universal/magazine-wide
Uses image-embed-card, metric-narrative, or comparison-twoCol instead of only basic cards
Keeps the result static and non-interactive
Does not require filter controls or event-bus behavior
